ks1.objective: Race around the circle, then throw the ball to hit the stumps in the middle.
successCriteria: Runs the full circle without cutting across

lks2.objective: Develop throwing accuracy under pressure — first to hit the stumps after a sprint scores 5 points.
successCriteria: Picks up the ball cleanly after the run
Aims body and feet at the target

uks2.objective: Refine throwing accuracy when tired — use stance and follow-through to hit the stumps despite the post-sprint rush.
successCriteria: Chooses overarm or underarm based on distance

space.description: A large circle (8–12m diameter) with the stumps in the centre. Children stand on cones around the outside.

steps: Create a circle with a set of stumps in the middle.
Children start from a cone on the outside of the circle.
On "go" children race around the circle and back to their starting position.
Once returned they pick up the ball and try to hit the stumps.
First child to hit the stumps scores 5 points.
questions: What part of your body do you use to aim your throw?
What is the best way to throw a ball for longer distances?
What is the best way to throw a ball for shorter distances?

step.space: Make the circle bigger or smaller
step.equipment: Reduce stumps or change the target
step.people: Pair children up and have them back each other up on the throw

errors.issue: Throwing too hard for the partner
errors.fix: Reward accuracy: '10 clean catches at YOUR distance'

errors.issue: Reaching with arms instead of moving feet
errors.fix: Cue 'feet to the ball, not hands to the ball'
